title: centaurea infestans
intro: Centaurea infestans, also known as the white spotty knapweed, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Centaurea infestans include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Centaurea infestans can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be consumed or applied topically to utilize its medicinal properties.
